src_raw: One of the HKMA’s most important tasks is to manage risks to the monetary and banking systems.  Risk management is undertaken both at a working level in the day-to-day operations and at a higher level through strategic planning. Under the HKMA’s risk management framework, there are two high-level committees — the Macro Surveillance Committee and the Risk Committee — both of which are chaired by the Chief Executive of the HKMA.
mt_raw: 金管局最重要的任務之一是管理對貨幣及銀行體系的風險。風險管理既在日常運作中的工作層面進行，也在戰略規劃的更高層面進行。根據金管局的風險管理架構，設有兩個高層委員會——宏觀監察委員會及風險委員會，均由金管局總裁擔任主席。

src_raw: A robust operational risk management framework is in place covering organisational risks at two levels: the entity level and the department level.  Entity-level risks refer mainly to those which concern the entire organisation in the medium term, or which might call for a cross-departmental response. Potential and emerging risks identified by business units, and the adequacy of the control measures and mitigating strategies they devise, are reviewed and reported every quarter.  This is supplemented by a top-down approach to manage entity-level risks, in which senior colleagues heading different business units actively identify risks of wider impact and propose mitigating measures.  The Risk Committee discusses these assessments and decides on appropriate follow-up actions.
mt_raw: 一個健全的操作風險管理框架已經建立，涵蓋機構層面和部門層面的風險。機構層面的風險主要指那些涉及整個組織中期目標，或者需要跨部門回應的風險。業務單位識別的潛在和新興風險，以及他們制定的控制措施和緩解策略的充分性，每季度都會被審查和報告。這補充了自上而下的機構層面風險管理方法，其中各業務單位的高級同事主動識別具有廣泛影響的風險並提出緩解措施。風險委員會將討論這些評估結果並決定適當的跟進行動。

src_raw: Climate change is one of the most pressing challenges facing the world today.  To combat climate change, countries agreed, under the Paris Agreement1, to take actions to cut greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions and eventually achieve carbon neutrality within this century.  Meeting the Paris Agreement climate change target will require investments of an estimated US$1–2 trillion a year globally2, making sustainability one of the biggest drivers of capital flows in the decades ahead.
mt_raw: 氣候變化是當今世界面臨最緊迫的挑戰之一。為應對氣候變化，各國根據《巴黎協定》同意採取行動減少溫室氣體（GHG）排放，最終實現本世紀內的碳中和。實現《巴黎協定》的氣候目標將需要全球每年約1萬至2萬億美元的投資，這使得可持續發展成為未來幾十年資本流動的主要驅動力之一。

src_raw: Hong Kong is determined to be at the forefront of this transition. mt_raw: 香港決心處於這一轉變的前沿。政府宣布將努力在2050年前實現碳中和，與全球先進經濟體保持一致。香港也是亞洲第一個承諾到2025年之前，在相關部門中具體與氣候相關財務披露工作組（TCFD）建議相一致的司法管轄區。

src_raw: To the financial sector, climate change is both a source of risk and opportunity. mt_raw: 對金融業而言，氣候變化既是風險來源，也是機會。雖然金融業無法避免氣候變化帶來的威脅，但它在轉變過程中扮演關鍵角色，通過提供更多資金支持符合低溫室氣體排放和氣候應變發展路徑的活動。

src_raw: First, as a banking supervisor, the HKMA aims to strengthen resilience of the banking system against climate risks and to accelerate banks’ efforts in supporting the transition to a low-carbon economy.  To accomplish this, the HKMA has formulated a regulatory framework setting out supervisory requirements for banks to manage and disclose climate risks.  A pilot exercise on climate risk stress test to assess the resilience of the banking sector was also concluded successfully in 2021.  Meanwhile, on an ongoing basis, the HKMA takes into account climate change in monitoring financial stability risks.
mt_raw: 首先，作為銀行業監管機構，金管局旨在加強銀行體系對氣候風險的抗壓能力，加速銀行支持向低碳經濟過渡的努力。為達成此目標，金管局制定了監管框架，設定了銀行管理與披露氣候風險的監管要求。2021年成功完成了一項針對銀行業氣候風險壓力測試的試驗。同時，金管局持續在監測金融穩定風險時考慮氣候變化因素。

src_raw: Secondly, in its market development role, the HKMA is focusing its efforts in enhancing Hong Kong’s role as a green and sustainable finance hub in the region, including the Greater Bay Area (GBA).  Hong Kong, being Mainland’s offshore financial centre, is well-positioned to channel global investments into Mainland’s plans to achieve peak carbon emissions by 2030 and carbon neutrality by 2060.  The HKMA is also working through the Green and Sustainable Finance Cross-Agency Steering Group (CASG)3, which the HKMA co-chairs, to build industry capacity, address data gaps and explore the development of a local green classification framework.
mt_raw: 其次，在市場發展方面，金管局重點提升香港在該地區，包括大灣區（GBA）內作為綠色和可持續金融中心的地位。作為內地的離岸金融中心，香港具備良好條件，可以將全球投資引導至內地實現2030年碳達峰和2060年碳中和的計劃。金管局還透過由其共同主持的綠色和可持續金融跨機構督導小組（CASG），與業界合作提高產業能力，解決數據缺口，探索本地綠色分類框架的發展。

src_raw: In fact, Hong Kong is a leader in green finance solutions, having established the world’s first government Global Medium Term Note Programme dedicated to green bond issuances under the Government Green Bond Programme, and increased gradually the diversity of its issuances since 2019, both in terms of currencies and tenors.  To support the issuance of green and sustainable bonds and loans in the private sector, the Government set up the Green and Sustainable Finance Grant Scheme in May 2021. Administered by the HKMA, this scheme has incentivised more corporates to seek green and sustainable financing in Hong Kong, and encouraged more green advisers and external reviewers to establish or expand their presence here.
mt_raw: 實際上，香港在綠色金融解決方案方面處於領先地位，已建立了全球首個專門針對綠色債券發行的政府全球中期票據發行計劃，自2019年起逐步增加了發行種類的多樣性，包括貨幣和期限等方面。為了支持私人部門發行綠色和可持續債券和貸款，政府於2021年5月設立了綠色和可持續金融補貼計劃。由金管局負責管理，該計劃激勵更多企業在香港尋求綠色和可持續融資，並鼓勵更多綠色顧問公司和外部評審機構在此建立或擴展業務。

src_raw: Fourthly, the HKMA is stepping up its own green office initiatives to ensure its own operations conform to best practices in the management and mitigation of climate risks.  As a start, the HKMA has established its carbon profile across Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions4, and made corresponding disclosures.  At the same time, the HKMA has implemented measures to enhance industry-wide operational efficiency and minimise resource consumption.  In addition, the HKMA holds itself to high standards of corporate social responsibility (CSR) and works with its stakeholders to promote financial inclusion and financial literacy.
mt_raw: 第四，金管局正在加強自身的綠色辦公室措施，以確保其運作符合氣候風險管理和減緩的最佳實踐。作為起始步驟，金管局已在範疇1和範疇2排放量方面建立了碳輪廓，並作出相應的披露。同時，金管局實施了提高行業整體運營效率和減少資源消耗的措施。此外，金管局秉持高標準的機構社會責任（CSR），與其利益相關者合作，推動金融包容性和金融素養。

src_raw: In driving these sustainability initiatives and assessing climaterelated opportunities and risks, sustainability and green considerations are embedded in the HKMA’s governance and risk management frameworks, in which the leadership is actively involved in setting the overarching objectives and guiding principles, in consultation with the Exchange Fund Advisory Committee (EFAC) and its sub-committees. The HKMA’s CSR and Sustainability Committee (CSC) also assesses the potential impacts of sustainability and climate-related issues on the organisation, and devises strategies to reduce the impacts.  Execution of the relevant strategies and initiatives is closely monitored and reported to the top management regularly.  In addition, external consultants have been engaged to advise the HKMA in selected areas of work.
mt_raw: 在推動這些可持續發展措施並評估氣候相關的機會和風險時，可持續性和綠色考量被納入金管局的治理和風險管理架構中，其中領導層積極參與設定總體目標和指導原則，並與外匯基金諮詢委員會（EFAC）及其小組委員會進行磋商。金管局的機構社會責任與可持續發展委員會（CSC）也會評估可持續性和氣候相關問題對組織的潛在影響，並制定減少影響的策略。有關策略和措施的執行情況會定期向最高管理層報告。此外，金管局聘請了外部顧問在選定的工作領域提供建議。

src_raw: In December 2015, the Financial Stability Board created the TCFD to develop recommendations for more effective climate-related disclosures by companies to investors, lenders and insurance underwriters that could promote more informed decisions.  The TCFD’s recommendations are structured around four thematic areas that represent the core elements of how organisations operate: governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics and targets.
mt_raw: 2015年12月，金融穩定理事會成立了TCFD，以開發公司向投資者、貸款機構及保險承保商提供更有效的氣候相關披露建議。TCFD的建議涵蓋四個主題領域，代表組織運作的核心要素：管治、策略、風險管理，以及指標及目標。

src_raw: In 2020, the HKMA developed a common framework for assessing the “greenness baseline” of individual banks in collaboration with the industry.  The HKMA also conducted the first greenness assessment of banks, which marked the completion of Phase I.  To help banks prepare for the new supervisory requirements, a white paper was published in June 2020, which outlined the HKMA’s initial thinking on supervisory expectations for climate risk management.
mt_raw: 2020年，金管局與業界合作，制定了評估單個銀行「綠色基準」的共同框架。金管局還進行了首次銀行綠色評估，標誌著第一階段的完成。為幫助銀行準備新的監管要求，2020年6月發表了一份白皮書，概述了金管局對氣候風險管理監管期望的初步想法。

src_raw: In December 2021, following an industry consultation, the HKMA issued the supervisory requirements for banks to incorporate climate considerations into governance, strategy, risk management and disclosure.  In particular, specific requirements and concrete timeline were set out in mandating climate-related disclosures based on the recommendations of the TCFD before 2025. The requirements will set a benchmark to help banks upgrade their risk management capability.
mt_raw: 2021年12月，在徵詢業界意見後，金管局發佈了銀行將氣候因素納入管治、策略、風險管理和披露的監管要求。特別的是，根據TCFD的建議，在2025年前設定了具體的要求和具體時間表，以強制進行氣候相關披露。這些要求將設立基準，幫助銀行提升風險管理能力。

terms: {'ghg emission': '溫室氣體排放', 'hkma': '金管局', 'paris agreement': '巴黎協定', 'climate risk': '氣候風險', 'collective effort': '業界合作', 'global net zero emission': '全球淨零排放', 'portfolio alignment': '調整融資組合', 'risk management': '風險管理', 'risk management framework': '風險管理框架', 'sound practice': '良好做法'}
src_raw: Apart from incorporating climate risk considerations into the risk management framework, some banks have devoted resources and taken measures to support the goals of the Paris Agreement and contribute to achieving global net zero emissions.  The HKMA issued a circular in December 2021 to share with the industry the sound practices of these banks which would be of reference value to other banks in the industry in formulating their climate strategies.  These sound practices are grouped into four areas: reducing GHG emissions of the banks’ own operations, reducing financed emissions through portfolio alignment, assisting clients to transition, and promoting collective efforts to assist the economy to achieve a net-zero transition.
mt_raw: 除了將氣候風險考慮納入風險管理框架之外，一些銀行已經投入資源並採取措施支持《巴黎協定》的目標，並為實現全球淨零排放做出貢獻。金管局於2021年12月發佈了一封通告，與業界分享這些銀行的良好做法，這些做法對業界其他銀行制定氣候策略具有參考價值。這些良好做法分為四個方面：減少銀行自身運營中的溫室氣體排放、通過調整融資組合減少融資排放、協助客戶轉型，以及促進業界合作以協助經濟實現淨零轉型。

src_raw: To increase transparency across the financial markets and enable consistent policy making on green finance, the HKMA has been working with other financial regulators to explore a local green classification framework, which will facilitate navigation among the Common Ground Taxonomy (CGT) developed by the International Platform on Sustainable Finance (IPSF)8 and the green definitions adopted by China and European Union, for adoption across different financial sectors.  The work will be guided by the principles of interoperability, comparability and inclusiveness, taking into account other definitions of green, transitional activities and local considerations.  This initiative will reduce the risk of green washing, improve product comparability and ultimately enhance investor protection.
mt_raw: 為增加金融市場的透明度並促進綠色金融的統一政策制定，金管局正與其他金融監管機構合作，探索本地綠色分類框架，以便在不同金融部門採用國際可持續金融平台（IPSF）開發的共通綠色分類目錄（CGT）、中國和歐盟採用的綠色定義之間進行導航。該工作將以互換性、可比性及兼容性為指導原則，考慮其他綠色、過渡活動和本地因素的定義。此舉將降低漂綠風險，提高產品的可比性，最終增強投資者的保障。

src_raw: To assess the banking sector’s ability to cope with potential shocks brought about by climate change, the HKMA undertook a pilot exercise on climate risk stress test (CRST), which included the participation of 20 major retail banks and seven branches of international banking groups.  A report summarising the results of the pilot CRST exercise was published in December 2021.  The results showed that the banking sector remained resilient even in the face of extreme climate-related shocks, although these shocks could potentially give rise to significant adverse impacts on banks’ financials.  The CRST exercise helped to enhance banks’ capabilities for measuring and assessing climate risks. In light of the CRST results, the participating banks have devised plans to strengthen their climate strategies and risk governance frameworks.  The HKMA will continue to engage the industry to strengthen banks’ capability and enhance the CRST framework, taking into account the lessons learnt from the pilot exercise.
mt_raw: 為評估銀行業應對氣候變化帶來的潛在衝擊的能力，金管局進行了一項氣候風險壓力測試（CRST）試驗，其中包括20家主要零售銀行和7家國際銀行集團的分支機構參與。2021年12月發表了一份總結試驗結果的報告。結果顯示，即使面對極端氣候相關衝擊，銀行業仍保持彈性，儘管這些衝擊可能會對銀行的財務狀況產生重大不利影響。CRST試驗有助於提高銀行衡量和評估氣候風險的能力。根據CRST結果，參與銀行已制定計畫以加強其氣候策略和風險管治框架。金管局將繼續與業界合作，加強銀行的能力建設，並根據試驗經驗改進CRST框架。

mt_raw: 我確認已審核外匯基金及其附屬公司（「本集團」）於第217至319頁所載的財務報表，該等財務報表包括截至2021年12月31日止年度的外匯基金及本集團資產負債表，以及該年度的收支帳戶、綜合收益表、股東權益變動表及現金流量表，以及財務報表附註，包括主要會計政策摘要。

src_raw: In my opinion, the financial statements give a true and fair view of the financial position of the Fund and of the Group as at 31 December 2021, and of their financial performance and cash flows for the year then ended in accordance with Hong Kong Financial Reporting Standards (“HKFRSs”) issued by the Hong Kong Institute of Certified Public Accountants (“HKICPA”) and have been properly prepared in accordance with the directive of the Chief Executive made under section 7 of the Exchange Fund Ordinance (Cap. 66).
mt_raw: 根據香港會計師公會（Hong Kong Institute of Certified Public Accountants，簡稱HKICPA）頒發的香港財務報告準則（Hong Kong Financial Reporting Standards，簡稱HKFRSs），並按照行政長官根據《外匯基金條例》（第66章）第7條作出的指示，我認為上述財務報表真實且公平地反映了外匯基金及本集團截至2021年12月31日的財務狀況，以及該年度的財務表現及現金流量。

src_raw: I conducted my audit in accordance with the directive of the Chief Executive made under section 7 of the Exchange Fund Ordinance and the Audit Commission auditing standards.  My responsibilities under those standards are further described in the Auditor’s responsibilities for the audit of the financial statements section of my report.  I am independent of the Group in accordance with those standards, and I have fulfilled my other ethical responsibilities in accordance with those standards.  I believe that the audit evidence I have obtained is sufficient and appropriate to provide a basis for my opinion.
mt_raw: 我的審計是根據《外匯基金條例》第7條下行政長官的指示及審計署的審計準則進行的。我在這些準則下的責任進一步說明於本報告中「審計師就財務報表審計而須承擔的責任」部分。我與本集團獨立，並按照這些準則履行其他道德責任。我相信所取得的審計證據足夠適當，足以作為意見基礎。

src_raw: The Group’s investment properties were stated at their fair values, totalling HK$27,089 million as at 31 December 2021. Such valuations involved significant judgments and estimates, including the valuation methodologies and the assumptions used.

src_raw: In connection with my audit of the financial statements, my responsibility is to read the other information and, in doing so, consider whether the other information is materially inconsistent with the financial statements or my knowledge obtained in the audit or otherwise appears to be materially misstated.  If, based on the work I have performed, I conclude that there is a material misstatement of this other information, I am required to report that fact.  I have nothing to report in this regard.
mt_raw: 就我對財務報表的審計而言，我的責任是閱讀其他資訊，並在此過程中考慮其他資訊是否與財務報表或我在審計中獲得的知識有重大不一致之處，或者似乎存在重大誤述。如果根據我已完成的工作，我認為其他資訊存在重大誤述，我必須報告此事實。在這方面，我沒有需要報告的內容。

src_raw: The Monetary Authority is responsible for the preparation of the financial statements that give a true and fair view in accordance with HKFRSs issued by the HKICPA and the directive of the Chief Executive made under section 7 of the Exchange Fund Ordinance, and for such internal control as the Monetary Authority determines is necessary to enable the preparation of financial statements that are free from material misstatement, whether due to fraud or error.
mt_raw: 金融管理專員負責編製符合香港會計師公會頒發的香港財務報告準則（HKFRSs）及行政長官根據《外匯基金條例》（第66章）第7條作出的指示的財務報表，並負責建立必要的內部控制，以便編製無重大錯誤陳述的財務報表（無論是因舞弊還是錯誤所致）。

terms: {'audit commission': '審計署', 'audit commission auditing standard': '審計署的審計準則', 'financial statement': '財務報表', 'material misstatement': '重大錯誤陳述'}
src_raw: My objectives are to obtain reasonable assurance about whether the financial statements as a whole are free from material misstatement, whether due to fraud or error, and to issue an auditor’s report that includes my opinion.  Reasonable assurance is a high level of assurance, but is not a guarantee that an audit conducted in accordance with the Audit Commission auditing standards will always detect a material misstatement when it exists.  Misstatements can arise from fraud or error and are considered material if, individually or in the aggregate, they could reasonably be expected to influence the economic decisions of users taken on the basis of these financial statements.
mt_raw: 我的目標是獲取合理保證，以確定財務報表整體是否存在重大錯誤陳述（無論是因舞弊還是錯誤所致），並發佈包含我的意見的審計師報告。合理保證是一種高程度的保證，但不是一種保證，即根據審計署的審計準則進行的審計總是能發現存在的重大錯誤陳述。錯誤陳述可能因舞弊或錯誤產生，若個別或合計起來可能合理預期影響使用者根據這些財務報表做出的經濟決策。

terms: {'monetary authority': '金融管理專員', 'accounting estimate': '會計估計', 'accounting policy': '會計政策', 'audit evidence': '審計憑證', 'audit procedure': '審計程序', 'financial statement': '財務報表', 'go concern': '持續經營', 'go concern basis of accounting': '持續經營作為會計基礎', 'internal control': '內部監控', 'material misstatement': '重大錯誤陳述', 'material uncertainty': '重大不確定性'}
mt_raw:
- 識別和評估財務報表的重大錯誤陳述風險（無論是因舞弊還是錯誤所致），設計和執行針對這些風險的審計程序，並取得足夠且適當的審計證據以作為意見基礎。由於舞弊可能涉及共謀、偽造、故意遺漏、虛構或違反內部控制，因此未能發現因舞弊導致的重大錯誤陳述的風險比因錯誤導致的風險更高；
- 為了設計適合情況的審計程序，需了解與審計相關的內部控制，但這並非為了對本集團的內部控制有效性發表意見；
- 評估金融管理專員所採用的會計政策的適當性和會計估計及相關披露的合理性；
- 就金融管理專員採用持續經營會計基礎的適當性作出結論，並根據審計證據評估是否存在與可能對本集團持續經營能力造成重大懷疑的事件或條件相關的重大不確定性。如果我認為存在重大不確定性，我必須在審計師報告中指出財務報表中的相關披露，或如果這些披露不足，則修改我的意見。我的結論是根據審計證據得出的，直至審計師報告日期。然而，未來的事件或條件可能會導致本集團無法繼續經營；
- 評估財務報表的整體呈報、結構和內容，包括披露，並確定財務報表是否以實現公平陳述的方式反映基礎交易和事件；以及
- 取得足夠且適當的審計證據，關於集團內實體或業務活動的財務資訊，以表達對財務報表的意見。我對集團審計的方向、監督和執行

terms: {'exchange fund': '外匯基金', 'exchange fund advisory committee': '外匯基金諮詢委員會', 'the exchange fund': '外匯基金', 'financial statement': '財務報表', 'key audit matter': '關鍵審計事項'}
src_raw: From the matters communicated with the Audit Sub-Committee of the Exchange Fund Advisory Committee, I determine those matters that were of most significance in the audit of the financial statements of the current period and are therefore the key audit matters.  I describe these matters in my auditor’s report unless law or regulation precludes public disclosure about the matter or when, in extremely rare circumstances, I determine that a matter should not be communicated in my report because the adverse consequences of doing so would reasonably be expected to outweigh the public interest benefits of such communication.
mt_raw: 從與外匯基金諮詢委員會審核委員會的溝通中，我確定那些對當前期間財務報表審計最具重要性的事項，因此成為關鍵審計事項。除非法律法規禁止公開披露該事項，或在極端罕見的情況下，我決定該事項不應在報告中披露，因為這樣做的不利後果很可能超過公開披露的公共利益好處，否則我將在審計師報告中描述這些事項。
